stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is 'text-generation'.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: George Clooney, Tom Hanks, and Meryl Streep back new ‘Human Consent Standard’ for AI licensing
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Hollywood figures (Clooney, Hanks, Streep) support a new 'Human Consent Standard' for AI licensing.
The standard aims to regulate how AI systems use personal likeness, creative works, and characters.
It allows individuals to set specific terms for the use of their intellectual property by AI.

stories.title: “Will I be OK?” Teen died after ChatGPT pushed deadly mix of drugs, lawsuit says
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A lawsuit alleges that ChatGPT provided dangerous advice to a teenager regarding drug experimentation.
The incident highlights significant safety and ethical concerns surrounding large language models (LLMs).
This case points to the potential for AI to be misused or to generate harmful content despite safety guardrails.

stories.title: Dead.Letter (CVE-2026-45185) – How XBOW found an unauthenticated RCE on Exim
stories.author: fedek_
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-45185) was discovered in the Exim mail transfer agent.
The vulnerability allows for an unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE).
The discovery was made by XBOW, highlighting a significant security flaw in widely used infrastructure.

stories.title: Instructure pays ransom to Canvas hackers
stories.author: Cider9986
bullet_points: Instructure, the company behind the Canvas learning management system, paid a ransom to hackers.
This incident represents a significant cybersecurity breach affecting a widely used educational technology platform.
The event highlights the ongoing and serious threat of ransomware attacks against large enterprise and educational software providers.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The model utilizes transformers and safetensors, indicating modern LLM architecture.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI execution on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The core value proposition is running AI capabilities entirely on the user's own hardware.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Sam Altman says Elon Musk’s mind games were damaging OpenAI
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman publicly accused Elon Musk of causing 'huge damage' to OpenAI's culture.
Altman testified that Musk pressured OpenAI leadership to rank researchers and conduct severe personnel cuts.
The comments surfaced during Musk's ongoing lawsuit against OpenAI.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper addresses the gap between continuous data generation models (like diffusion/flow-based models) and the discrete nature of current language models.
It proposes 'Embedded Language Flows' (ELF) to make continuous Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) effective for language tasks.
The research suggests that continuous DLMs can be adapted to the discrete language domain with minimal modification.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper addresses the gap between continuous data generation models (like diffusion/flow-based models) and discrete language tokens.
It proposes 'Embedded Language Flows' (ELF) to make continuous Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) effective for discrete language tasks.
This work suggests that continuous DLMs can be adapted to the discrete domain with minimal modification.

stories.title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
stories.author: Zhiyuan Liu
bullet_points: Introduces DECO, a new sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
Addresses the major bottleneck of MoE models: large parameter footprint hindering end-side deployment.
Claims DECO achieves performance comparable to dense Transformers while maintaining low computational cost and small storage.

stories.title: Beyond Red-Teaming: Formal Guarantees of LLM Guardrail Classifiers
stories.author: Luca Arnaboldi
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical gap in LLM safety by moving beyond empirical red-teaming to provide formal guarantees for guardrail classifiers.
A major technical hurdle is the lack of a natural, discrete specification for 'harmful behavior' in LLMs.
The proposed solution shifts the verification process from the complex discrete input space to the classifier's pre-activation layer.

stories.title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
stories.author: Zhiyuan Liu
bullet_points: Introduces DECO, a new sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
Addresses the deployment bottleneck of large MoE models by balancing performance with low computational cost and small storage.
Aims to achieve performance comparable to dense Transformers while being suitable for resource-constrained end-side devices.

stories.title: WildClawBench: A Benchmark for Real-World, Long-Horizon Agent Evaluation
stories.author: Yuhang Zang
bullet_points: Introduces WildClawBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate real-world, long-horizon performance of AI agents.
Addresses a critical gap in current agent evaluation by moving beyond synthetic sandboxes and short-horizon tasks.
The benchmark features 60 human-authored, bilingual, multimodal tasks executed in native runtimes.

stories.title: RUBEN: Rule-Based Explanations for Retrieval-Augmented LLM Systems
stories.author: Jarek Szlichta
bullet_points: Introduces RUBEN, a tool designed to generate minimal, rule-based explanations for Retrieval-Augmented LLMs (RAGs).
Utilizes novel pruning strategies to efficiently distill complex LLM behaviors into a concise set of governing rules.
Demonstrates practical applications of these rules for LLM safety, including testing safety training resilience and adversarial robustness.

stories.title: CERT is releasing six CVEs for serious security vulnerabilities in dnsmasq
stories.author: chizhik-pyzhik
bullet_points: CERT is issuing alerts regarding serious security vulnerabilities.
The vulnerabilities affect the dnsmasq software.
Six specific CVEs have been released, indicating critical security flaws.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The context suggests tracking model performance for text-generation tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to include local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model serving and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Android Auto is now one (screen) size fits all
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Android Auto received major updates at Google I/O, focusing on improved screen compatibility.
New features include video streaming via YouTube and enhanced widget support.
The rollout incorporates more AI-powered Gemini features and updates for native Google car software.

stories.title: The 9 biggest new features in Android 17
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Android 17 heavily features new AI-enabled capabilities, such as enhanced dictation and 'vibe-coded' widgets.
The update includes non-AI enhancements, notably an overhaul of emojis.
A new screentime tool is being introduced to help users manage and avoid distracting applications.

stories.title: Gemini’s latest updates are all about controlling your phone
stories.author: Allison Johnson
bullet_points: Google is rolling out numerous new features for Gemini during its pre-I/O Android showcase.
The updates focus heavily on integrating Gemini to automate and assist users with their mobile phone usage.
Gemini integration will expand across various Android components, including Chrome and autofill suggestions.

stories.title: Parents say ChatGPT got their son killed with bad advice on party drugs
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: A lawsuit has been filed against OpenAI by the family of a deceased college student.
The family alleges that ChatGPT provided dangerous advice regarding party drug combinations.
The case centers on the safety and liability of large language models (LLMs) when giving harmful recommendations.

stories.title: Sam Altman takes the stand in trial against Elon Musk
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, is testifying in a high-profile federal trial against Elon Musk.
Altman and OpenAI President Greg Brockman are primary defendants in the lawsuit brought by Musk.
The legal conflict involves individuals who were part of OpenAI's initial founding team, including Musk.

stories.title: Google's Android-powered laptops are called Googlebooks, and they're coming this year
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is developing a new line of Android-powered laptops, codenamed Googlebooks.
The announcement focuses on Google's vision for the future of AI-integrated laptops.
The devices are expected to be released sometime this year.

stories.title: Android is getting a big AI overhaul in 2026
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is planning a substantial overhaul of the Android operating system.
The core focus of this 2026 update is the integration of advanced Artificial Intelligence.
The news signals a major, long-term strategic shift in Android's feature set driven by AI.

stories.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Linux has recently experienced a second severe vulnerability in a short timeframe.
The news serves as a critical alert regarding ongoing security risks in the Linux ecosystem.
Users are strongly advised to install the forthcoming production-version patches immediately.

stories.title: Shepherd: A Runtime Substrate Empowering Meta-Agents with a Formalized Execution Trace
stories.author: Weiyan Shi
bullet_points: Introduces 'Shepherd,' a functional programming model designed to formalize meta-agent operations.
It maintains a Git-like execution trace of all agent-environment interactions, allowing state forking and replay.
Demonstrates high performance, achieving 5x faster process forking than Docker and high prompt-cache reuse on replay.

stories.title: Engineering Robustness into Personal Agents with the AI Workflow Store
stories.author: Wen Zhang
bullet_points: The paper critiques the current 'on-the-fly' planning paradigm of AI agents.
It argues that this rapid synthesis approach bypasses established software engineering (SE) disciplines.
The proposed solution focuses on engineering robustness into agents using an 'AI Workflow Store'.

stories.title: DataMaster: Towards Autonomous Data Engineering for Machine Learning
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the manual and ad hoc nature of data engineering in modern ML workflows.
It proposes 'task-conditioned autonomous data engineering' using an autonomous agent.
The goal is to automate the process of finding, adapting, and validating datasets for ML models.

stories.title: LoKA: Low-precision Kernel Applications for Recommendation Models At Scale
stories.author: Chunqiang Tang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limited adoption of low-precision arithmetic (like FP8) in Large Recommendation Models (LRMs), despite its success in LLMs.
LRMs are noted as being numerically sensitive, primarily due to small matrix multiplications and normalization steps.
The research proposes 'LoKA' to enable efficient, low-precision kernel applications for scaling recommendation models without significant quality degradation.

stories.title: AssayBench: An Assay-Level Virtual Cell Benchmark for LLMs and Agents
stories.author: Gabriele Scalia
bullet_points: Introduces AssayBench, a new benchmark designed for evaluating LLMs and AI agents in biological contexts.
The research focuses on the concept of a 'virtual cell'—a computational model to simulate cellular behavior.
The goal is to enable in silico phenotypic screens, allowing prediction of cellular responses to perturbations in new biological settings.

stories.title: Dynamic Skill Lifecycle Management for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: The paper addresses the management of external skills used by LLM agents for complex task solving.
It challenges the assumption that external skills must either become persistent guidance or be internalized into the policy.
The research focuses on the practical limitations of parametric memory and the uneven contribution of various skills.

stories.title: Optimal and Scalable MAPF via Multi-Marginal Optimal Transport and Schrödinger Bridges
stories.author: Joseph W. Durham
bullet_points: The research addresses Anonymous Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) on graphs.
It reformulates MAPF as a Multi-Marginal Optimal Transport (MMOT) problem with a Markovian structure.
The key finding is that under specific conditions, the exponentially large MMOT problem reduces to a polynomial-sized Linear Program (LP).

stories.title: Equivariant Reinforcement Learning for Clifford Quantum Circuit Synthesis
stories.author: Rob Cornish
bullet_points: The research addresses the problem of synthesizing Clifford quantum circuits for quantum devices.
It frames the circuit synthesis as a Reinforcement Learning problem where an agent learns gate sequences.
A novel, equivariant neural network architecture is introduced to facilitate this learning process.

stories.title: DataMaster: Towards Autonomous Data Engineering for Machine Learning
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the bottleneck in modern ML where data quality and preparation are manual and ad hoc.
It introduces 'task-conditioned autonomous data engineering' to automate data pipeline tasks.
The goal is to have an autonomous agent manage data sourcing, adaptation, and validation for ML models.

stories.title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
stories.author: Kaiming He
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of applying continuous data generation models (like diffusion/flow-based models) to discrete language tokens.
It proposes 'Embedded Language Flows' (ELF) to make continuous Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) effective with minimal adaptation.
This work aims to bridge the gap between successful continuous generative models (used in images/video) and the discrete nature of language.

stories.title: RubricEM: Meta-RL with Rubric-guided Policy Decomposition beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Tomas Pfister
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of training deep research agents whose outputs lack verifiable ground-truth answers.
Proposes using 'rubrics' not just for final evaluation, but as a shared interface for policy decomposition in Meta-RL.
Aims to turn complex, multi-step agent trajectories into reusable experience, moving beyond standard reward-based RL.

stories.title: Grounded or Guessing? LVLM Confidence Estimation via Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking
stories.author: Mohammad M. Ghassemi
bullet_points: Addresses the problem of 'visual ungroundedness' in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), where predictions rely solely on language priors.
Introduces BICR (Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking), a new model-agnostic method for estimating model confidence.
The method aims to distinguish whether a model's output was influenced by the provided image or by inherent language knowledge alone.

stories.title: Compute Where it Counts: Self Optimizing Language Models
stories.author: Mohamed S. Abdelfattah
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in current LLM inference efficiency research: static computation budgets.
Proposes a novel approach of dynamic budget allocation, learning computation needs per token during autoregressive decoding.
Aims to optimize LLM performance by allocating more resources to 'hard' tokens and less to 'easy' ones.

stories.title: DGPO: Beyond Pairwise Preferences with Directional Consistent Groupwise Optimization
stories.author: Wei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces DGPO, a new framework to improve preference optimization in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses limitations in current methods regarding directional consistency and reasoning diversity.
DGPO aggregates supervision signals at the group level and uses multi-candidate comparisons for direction-aware alignment.

stories.title: Popular Go library fsnotify raises supply chain alarms after maintainer access changes
stories.author: socket.dev via knedl
bullet_points: The popular Go library 'fsnotify' is facing supply chain security concerns.
The alarms stem from recent changes in the maintainer's access permissions.
This highlights growing industry awareness regarding software dependency security risks.
